editions.title: A Friend Is Someone Who - A Children's Book About Friendship for Kids Ages 3-9 - Discover the Keys of Kindness to Making Friends, Being a Good Friend, & Growing Friendships

candidate.title: A Friend Is Someone Who - A Children's Book About Friendship for Kids Ages 3-9 - Discover the Keys of Kindness to Making Friends, Being a Good Friend, & Growing Friendships
candidate.description: Discover the true meaning of friendship - This charming picture book teaches kids how to be kind, supportive, and caring while building strong and lasting friendships. Perfect for ages 3-10 - With engaging rhymes and vibrant illustrations, young readers will learn valuable life lessons about making friends, being a good friend, and showing kindness to others. Encourages empathy and compassion - Children will understand the importance of kindness, loyalty, and helping others, reinforcing positive social skills for preschoolers, kindergarteners, and early elementary students. Beautifully illustrated and engaging - Every page is filled with colorful, heartwarming artwork that brings the story to life, making it a perfect bedtime story or classroom read-aloud. Great gift for kids - Ideal for birthdays, holidays, back-to-school, or just because! A must-have book to help kids navigate friendships and develop meaningful connections. Description A Friend Is Someone Who... is a heartwarming children's book that teaches kids the true meaning of friendship. Through delightful rhymes and vibrant illustrations, this beautifully crafted story helps children understand the importance of kindness, support, and being a good friend. In this engaging book, kids will discover how friends can bring joy, courage, and comfort in difficult times. Whether it's offering a hug, encouraging each other to reach their goals, or simply sharing a laugh, friendship is one of life's greatest treasures. This book also introduces the idea that friends come in all forms - from classmates and siblings to parents and even pets! Perfect for children ages 3-10, A Friend Is Someone Who... fosters social skills, empathy, and emotional intelligence. Parents, teachers, and caregivers can use this story to encourage meaningful conversations about kindness, respect, and how to build strong relationships. Ideal for bedtime reading, classrooms, or a thoughtful gift, this book will leave a lasting impression on young hearts. Help your child develop lifelong friendships with this inspiring and heartwarming read!

Beautifully illustrated and engaging - Every page is filled with colorful, heartwarming artwork that brings the story to life, making it a perfect bedtime story or classroom read-aloud. Great gift for kids - Ideal for birthdays, holidays, back-to-school, or just because! A must-have book to help kids navigate friendships and develop meaningful connections. Description A Friend Is Someone Who... is a heartwarming children's book that teaches kids the true meaning of friendship. Through delightful rhymes and vibrant illustrations, this beautifully crafted story helps children understand the importance of kindness, support, and being a good friend. In this engaging book, kids will discover how friends can bring joy, courage, and comfort in difficult times. Whether it's offering a hug, encouraging each other to reach their goals, or simply sharing a laugh, friendship is one of life's greatest treasures. This book also introduces the idea that friends come in all forms - from classmates and siblings to parents and even pets! Perfect for children ages 3-10, A Friend Is Someone Who... fosters social skills, empathy, and emotional intelligence. Parents, teachers, and caregivers can use this story to encourage meaningful conversations about kindness, respect, and how to build strong relationships. Ideal for bedtime reading, classrooms, or a thoughtful gift, this book will leave a lasting impression on young hearts. Help your child develop lifelong friendships with this inspiring and heartwarming read!

candidate.title: Stories, Time and Again
candidate.subtitle: A Program Guide for Schools and Libraries
candidate.description: Lively and imaginative book-based programs make it easy to engage young learners, while building their literacy and reading skills, and their love of books and reading. Your library or classroom will sizzle with excitement when you present these creative, book-based programs—and you just may have as much fun as the kids. Each of the ten chapters focuses on a popular theme or study area—Tropical Rainforests, Animals Down Under, In the Know (manners), and more—offering an annotated list of selected picture books and chapters books, and two complete programs with step-by-step instructions, materials lists, and all the reproducible patterns, scripts, and stories you'll need. Through reading, storytelling, puzzles, creative dramatics, writing exercises, arts and crafts, and more, you can engage young learners, while building their literacy and reading skills, and their love of books and reading. Children will delight in learning about amazing rainforest animals, performing a skit based on myths from ancient Egypt, writing their own fantasy stories, and holding a mouth-watering Medieval banquet. Designed for public and school libraries, these programs also fit beautifully into classroom studies. Grades K-6.

candidate.title: Saving Our Sons
candidate.subtitle: Raising Black Children in a Turbulent World
candidate.description: Raising Black Teen Boys in Turbulent Times "It is always heartening to see women step up to the writer's table. When the results are as adroit and affecting as Marita Golden's work, it is more than satisfying; it is a cause for celebration." —Toni Morrison, Nobel Laureate Two decades ago, Marita was the first Black writer to address the horrifying statistic that haunts all Black mothers: the leading cause of death among Black males under twenty-one is homicide. Today, police brutality rages on as millions call for the reformation of our broken law enforcement in the wake of the traumatic murders of Black teen boys like Trayvon Martin, Michael Brown and Daunte Wright. Read an intimate account of a mother’s efforts to save her son. Writing her son’s story against the backdrop of a society plagued by systemic racism, economic inequality, and mass incarceration, Golden offers a form of witness and testimony in a time of crisis for Black Americans. Learn how to grapple with the realities of Black America. Join Golden as she confronts the root causes of violence inflicted upon Black teen boys and reassesses the legacy of her own generation's struggle for civil rights. Explore Black boys’ difficult road to adulthood in the U.S. and learn why single Black mothers are often wrongly blamed for their sons’ actions. Gain invaluable advice and knowledge from trustworthy sources. In Saving Our Sons, Golden documents her conversations with psychologists, writers, and young Black males themselves. This book is designed to help you: Discuss and unpack generational trauma with loved ones Gain deeper insight into the injustices Black children face in the U.S. Recognize the importance of community for the success of Black teen boys If you liked Decoding Boys, Mother & Son: Our Back & Forth Journal, The Boy Crisis or Boy Mom, you’ll love Saving Our Sons.

candidate.title: Children’s Literature in Hitler’s Germany
candidate.subtitle: The Cultural Policy of National Socialism
candidate.description: Between 1933 and 1945, National Socialists enacted a focused effort to propagandize children’s literature by distorting existing German values and traditions with the aim of creating a homogenous “folk community.” A vast censorship committee in Berlin oversaw the publication, revision, and distribution of books and textbooks for young readers, exercising its control over library and bookstore content as well as over new manuscripts, so as to redirect the cultural consumption of the nation’s children. In particular, the Nazis emphasized Nordic myths and legends with a focus on the fighting spirit of the saga heroes, their community loyalty, and a fierce spirit of revenge—elements that were then applied to the concepts of loyalty to and sacrifice for the Führer and the fatherland. They also tolerated select popular series, even though these were meant to be replaced by modern Hitler Youth camping stories. In this important book, first published in 1984 and now back in print, Christa Kamenetsky demonstrates how Nazis used children’s literature to selectively shape a “Nordic Germanic” worldview that was intended to strengthen the German folk community, the Führer, and the fatherland by imposing a racial perspective on mankind. Their efforts corroded the last remnants of the Weimar Republic’s liberal education, while promoting an enthusiastic following for Hitler.

candidate.title: How Deep Is Your Love
candidate.description: A majestic picture book based on the Bee Gees's classic love song "How deep is your love? I really mean to learn 'Cause we're living in a world of fools Breaking us down When they all should let us be We belong to you and me . . ." How Deep Is Your Love is a fantastical picture book based on one of the Bee Gees's biggest hits. The song topped the Billboard Hot 100 chart, remaining in the top ten for seventeen weeks, and won the band's first of eight GRAMMY awards. With lyrics by the Bee Gees and illustrations by J.L. Meyer, the picture book tells the tale of two bunny mermaids who meet on a rock and descend into the water together. As the bunnies escape traps, skirt around nets and hooks, and protect each other in this thrilling adventure, they realize how deep their love is. Meyer's imaginative story compliments the hit song perfectly and will delight children and adults alike.

editions.title: Flovelys Children ́s Picture Book of love

candidate.title: Flovelys Children ́s Picture Book of love
candidate.subtitle: A children ́s picture story about friendship
candidate.description: Children's Book: Flovely ́s Children ́s Picture Book of love „Flovely loves you!“ is a lovingly illustrated Boost-book for young children. Clear images in flat design and simple rhymes that can easily be remembered and repeated make this book a constructive and cheerful children's book. The book tells three stories! This well-designed and inspiring children’s picture story, delivers recipe for becoming a good friend, and is an ideal picture book for the whole family or to read to your children at bedtime. This inspirational bedtime picture book for children ages 3-8, tells the story of true friendship. This picture book story is about Flovely, a cute creature from a beautiful country in the clouds. Friendship, love and well-being are important themes in this story. A recommended book, not only as a "bedtime story". In addition this book includes the story: “Flovely – Adventures in the Land of Castles”. Flovely appreciates the small reader to develop a positive self-awareness. This story may be ideal for reading to your kids at bedtime. It is suitable as a read-aloud book for preschoolers or as a beginning reader book for older children! Review: "helps to teach children an important social skill" "an entertaining way to teach children why they should forgive and respect others" About the author: The author Siegfried Freudenfels has been working as an illustrator for children's books for over 10 years. In 2010, his first free ebook, a new edition of famous children's classics, was published as a free children's book. The works soon became known and apart from further Good Night stories for children, children's games and lullabies appeared, also as free ebooks. The collection now comprises over 20 children's books for digital reading devices. More matching books by the author: "Adventures in Castleland - a bedtime picture book for boys and girls - a free ebook for kids" "Flovely on the flower meadow - A story about true friends and deep friendship - ebook for free" "Flovely Adventures on the houseboat - A funny story to read aloud or read to yourself - Read it free" These books can be found as free ebooks!

candidate.title: The Magic of Making Friends
candidate.subtitle: A Heartwarming Children's Picture Book about Kindness, Confidence, and Friendship - Social Skills and Empathy Story for Kids Ages 3-8
candidate.description: Shadow is a shy black puppy who just moved to a new neighborhood. Surrounded by playful kittens, bunnies, ducklings, and hedgehogs, Shadow feels alone-until a shimmering golden flower whispers, "Make a wish." With one simple wish-"I wish I had a friend"-Shadow's life begins to change. Through acts of kindness, sharing, and courage, Shadow learns the truth: the real magic of making friends isn't in flowers or wishes, but in being yourself and opening your heart.

candidate.title: The Seriously Silly 1-2-3 Book
candidate.subtitle: A Rhyming Picture Book for Kids Learning Their Numbers
candidate.description: "The Seriously Silly 1-2-3 Book" became an international bestseller within days of its publication. In it, children are introduced to the numbers one through twenty using colorful drawings and fun rhymes.After reading the brief rhyme on each page, you'll point out the featured number at the top of the page and encourage your child to find the other example of that same number on that page. Next, you'll challenge your child to find the numbers they've learned on previous pages and count them out beginning with one and working up to the featured number, making this book a fun interactive experience for both you and your child each time you share the book together.Every child will enjoy "The Seriously Silly 1-2-3 Book" whether they're learning their numbers or aren't quite there yet. The illustrations and rhymes will delight your child's eye, ear and funny bone while the learning experience will engage his or her mind.Many of Steve Hodge's rhyming picture books became #1 Bestsellers and several were chosen #1 “Hot New Release” within days of their publication. “The Seriously Silly 1-2-3 Book” is destined to become a favorite of children and the people they love for generations to come.

Next, you'll challenge your child to find the numbers they've learned on previous pages and count them out beginning with one and working up to the featured number, making this book a fun interactive experience for both you and your child each time you share the book together.Every child will enjoy "The Seriously Silly 1-2-3 Book" whether they're learning their numbers or aren't quite there yet. The illustrations and rhymes will delight your child's eye, ear and funny bone while the learning experience will engage his or her mind.Many of Steve Hodge's rhyming picture books became #1 Bestsellers and several were chosen #1 “Hot New Release” within days of their publication. “The Seriously Silly 1-2-3 Book” is destined to become a favorite of children and the people they love for generations to come.

candidate.title: Mortimer's Book of What-Ifs
candidate.subtitle: A Children's Rhyming Picture Book of Poetry
candidate.description: Children's (kids) Rhyming Picture Book with a Where's Waldo appeal. All kids ask "What if" questions each and every day. This is the way that they learn and develop their growing minds and also expand their vocabulary. This book is a collection of What if's asked by the beloved Mortimer Crump, the main character from the series: The Many Adventures of Mortimer Crump. This is a hilarious collection of questions and answers asked by Mortimer himself consisting of fun rhyming text and written in poetry with a Shel Silverstein feel. This book is sure to keep your kids entertained and laughing the whole way through and is fun to read over and over again. Questions such as: What if you had a remote control for people? (who wouldn't want one of these?) What if you hear a strange noise in the tub? What if you don't clean behind your ears? What if you have to get glasses? This book also has a Where's Waldo appeal as Mortimer is hidden on each and every page for the kids to find.

editions.title: Count to Ten With Your New Friends!

candidate.title: Count to Ten With Your New Friends!
candidate.subtitle: A Rhyming Picture Book for Kids Learning Their Numbers
candidate.description: "Count to Ten With Your New Friends" became a #1 Bestseller within days of its publication and introduces children to the numbers one through ten using colorful drawings, fantastic creatures and fun rhymes. You'll read the rhyme on each page then point out the featured number on that page. Next, you'll encourage your child to find that featured number as well as all the numbers he or she has learned on previous pages and count them out beginning with one and working up to the featured number, making this book a fun, interactive experience for both you and your child each time you share the book together. "Count to Ten With Your New Friends" is destined to become a favorite of children and the people they love for generations to come.

candidate.title: What a Funny Little Duck
candidate.subtitle: My First Rhyming Picture Book Perfect for Ages 3-5.
candidate.description: A book with bright, exciting and funny illustrations and expressive, rhyming text that your children will want to read again and again - singing to themselves the wonderful rhymes of small and easy to remember rhymes. A curious little duckling starts his day with a rhyme. Traveling on a small boat or inhaling the smell of flowers in a flower meadow, chasing butterflies and telling funny stories to his friends. A funny little duckling can do everything. He can rhyme anything. Easy rhymes for little children. Quick to remember and easy to read. Spend an unforgettable evening and read together with your children. - Why Parents & Educators will Love It: Increases awareness and fun vocabulary in your child Promotes kindness, joy, and good humor to you and your children Perfect for children learning to read short and easy stories or SEL activities with your children Written in easy, poetic language that is well suited for ages 3-5 Rhymes with easy rhythm and rhyme that children love to read on their own or hear aloud - Why Kids Love It: Short, easy-to-read, charming adventures about a funny Duckling will resonate with young readers. Each colorful picture gives a desire for exploration, discovery, wonder to young children and many more.

candidate.title: Everything Will Be Ok
candidate.subtitle: A Rhyming Picture Book for Kids Which Encourages Children to Stay Strong, Have Courage and Never Give Up in Times of Adversity, Anger, Depression and
candidate.description: "If you are feeling down," "Don't worry" "No need to frown." "Everything will be OK." Throughout this book one courageous girl encourages everyone to carry on no matter what trouble they may be facing. Everyone faces tough times. This book inspires children with a message of strength, acceptance and perseverance. Everything will Be OK is illustrated with a unique combination of photography taken in upstate New York and graphic design. The non-wordy rhyming style of this book makes it a perfect addition to any read aloud. **This book has been revised with enhanced illustration by the author in June 2017*** For more information visit the Author Page at: www.Deesignery.com

candidate.title: The King's Party
candidate.subtitle: A Beautifully Illustrated, Rhyming Picture Book for Children of All Ages
candidate.description: The King's Party by BobbiCat is the story of a young girl who has nothing pretty to wear to the King's Party. She is heart-broken until her animal friends come to her rescue.-Vibrant, color illustrations will captivate your young child.-Rhyming lines help engage your child and sustain interest.-Your child will love to read this story over and over with you.Your younger children will enjoy the illustrations and sing-song tone of the story while your older children will particularly like the poignant rhymes.The brightly colored illustrations will teach your child the story, and soon they will be able to locate the correct word and read it themselves.This story will be enjoyed by children of all ages.

candidate.title: Sally's Silly Hair Day
candidate.subtitle: A Rhyming Children's Picture Book ( Fun Ebooks for Kids )
candidate.description: "I've reviewed a number of children's picture books, many of them quite good, but this is one of the most creative ones that I've read in a while." - J. Chambers Amazon Top 50 Reviewer "There are amazing lessons in this book, one of which is the importance of thinking outside the box as well as loving you for you." - C.A. Webb Amazon Top 1000 Reviewer "Both of my granddaughters loved this book. We had to read it twice." - Nancy "Had my grand kids in stitches!" - Kitchenella "OMG - This Book Is SOOO Much Fun!!" - Cathy "The illustrations are hilarious, and the rhyming makes it very easy to read. I give it 5 stars!" - Shawn "The images are spectacular and very well done. My niece read this three times already! Recommended!" - Robert Best selling children's book authors Mark Smith & Julie Richmond team up to bring you one of the cutest, silliest rhyming picture books that every little girl is sure to love. This fully illustrated picture book comes to life with bright vibrant colors, and illustrations that will make you laugh. Sally's Silly Hair Day is a silly rhyming picture book for girls age 3-9, but the kid in all of us will laugh and giggle right along with our children. "What if I was a princess with hair that was shiny and bright? My hair would be full of jewels that sparkle with delight." "What would my hair look like if I lived in the sky? It would be white fluffy clouds, and I would float right by." What other types of silly hair does Sally think of? Pick up a copy of this funny picture book and find out for yourself!

candidate.title: The Tickle Monster
candidate.subtitle: Rhyming Picture Book for Children
candidate.description: The Tickle Monster Arnie Lightning has created an instant classic with The Tickle Monster. This is a beautifully crafted picture book that children will LOVE! It is perfect for as a bedtime story or reading aloud with family and friends. The rhymes are very cute and the illustrations are even better! Perfect for toddlers, early readers, and as a bedtime story. Fully illustrated picture book Rhyming bedtime story Great for toddlers and younger children From the book... It begins with one finger. Then maybe a toe. Maybe two toes, you know. Pretty soon, sure as the winds blow. The Tickle Monster brings laughs that linger. He is known as the laugh bringer. Best-Selling Children's Book Author - Arnie Lightning Arnie Lightning is a best-selling children's book author with a straightforward goal. He wants his work to create a positive impact in the lives of others through children's books. Learning morals, lessons, and good character can start at a young age. Arnie's books reflect this. By providing a comfortable and entertaining environment, learning can be a fun activity! Scroll up and click 'buy' to spend some quality time with your child!

candidate.title: ABC of Things that Spin
candidate.subtitle: A Rhyming Children's Picture Book
candidate.description: Axles, baby mobiles, cement trucks, and more. There's so much fun to explore! Enjoy this easy-to-read children's picture book especially for kids who love things that spin! Full of all the things that turn and twirl, kids will discover all the special things that spin from A to Z. With bright and cheery pictures to go along with the easy-to-read text, this book is fun to read over and over again.

candidate.title: How to Write a Children's Picture Book
candidate.subtitle: Tips and Tricks for Writing Illustrated Picture Books for Kids
candidate.description: WRITING FOR CHILDREN **UPDATED New Chapter: How to write a metafiction picture book** Kids and books—they just go together. Children’s picture books are an addictive passion! You love reading to kids and wish that you could read one of your own. Now, you can make that dream a reality. Writing illustrated picture books for children can seem deceptively simple. You must tell a complete story in less than 1000 words, and less than 350 words is best. Characters grow and change and keep the reader emotionally invested, while also giving the illustrator great possibilities for the art. It’s a balancing act all the way around. Writing teacher and author of over a dozen children’s books Darcy Pattison explains the craft of writing children’s picture books. You’ll learn why format matters, how to appeal to kids and parents, how to write a read-aloud friendly book, and much more. Pattison provides tips on writing, editing, and marketing your manuscript to publishers. If you like detailed writing guides, plenty of contemporary examples, and practical worksheets, then you’ll love Darcy Pattison’s guidance on creating your own masterpiece that will fascinate generations of young readers.

candidate.title: Granddad's Picture Book
candidate.subtitle: A Beautifully Illustrated, Rhyming Picture Book for Children of All Ages
candidate.description: Granddad's Picture Book is the beautiful story of a grandfather's story time that will take the reader back in time and to fantasy lands of imagination. Your child will dance with leprechauns, swim with mermaids, and have tea with fairies along with other wondrous adventures. --Vibrant, color illustrations will captivate your young child. --Rhyming lines help engage your child and sustain interest. --Your child will love to read this story over and over with you. Your younger children will enjoy the illustrations and sing-song tone of the story while your older children will particularly like the poignant rhymes. The brightly colored illustrations will teach your child the story, and soon they will be able to locate the correct word and read it themselves. This story will be enjoyed by children of all ages.

candidate.title: The Day My Mommy Quit!
candidate.subtitle: Funny Rhyming Picture Book for Beginner Readers (Ages 2-8)
candidate.description: The Day My Mommy Quit! Funny Rhyming Picture Book for Beginner Readers (Suitable for ages 2-8) Beautifully Illustrated Story that will have both parents and children laughing out loud! Find out what happens to this family when Mom gets fed up and decides to stop doing all the chores. How will the family cope when they are so used to Mom doing so much for them? What happens next is hilarious, but also includes a valuable lesson for the whole family, even the cat and dog! What would you do if your Mommy quit on you? You and your children will love how the family resolves this issue in this adorable and humorous tale. Scroll up and order your copy now! Receive the kindle version FREE when ordering your print copy.

candidate.title: What to Bee?
candidate.subtitle: A Rhyming Picture Book for Children That Encourages Good Virtues, Values and Behavior
candidate.description: Excerpt from the Book: "Be friendly and treat others with care." "Happiness is amazing to share." "Be polite wherever you go." "Your good manners are great to show." A young bee encourages children to embrace good virtues and to respect both themselves and others. Join this Bee as he has fun soaring through Bee-ville's neighborhood and local playground. This book is written in a fun rhyming style which is sure to engage young readers during story times or read alouds. Visit the author page at www.Deesignery.com

candidate.title: The Circus is Coming to Town
candidate.subtitle: A Beautifully Illustrated, Rhyming Picture Book for Children of All Ages
candidate.description: BobbiCat transports your child to the bright lights of pure delight under the big top in "The Circus is Coming to Town". There, among the aroma of popcorn, peanuts, cotton candy, and soft drinks, they will see amazing acrobats, and brave trapeze artists that tantalize the senses with death-defying acts of entertainment. Give your child the experience of tigers, elephants, and clowns, oh, my and much, much more in this delightful rhyming book filled with fantastic, colorful, illustrations that will entertain them for hours on end. candidate.subtitle: A Fun Rhyming Picture Book of Numbers

With catchy rhymes and adorable illustrations, your child's imagination will be captivated. This is another charming book from author, Josselin Budd that will have your kids counting the minutes 'til they can read it again.

candidate.title: An Unlikely Friendship
candidate.subtitle: A Rhyming Picture Book Story for Children Ages 3-6
candidate.description: Settle down for story time with this fun rhyming tale for kids! Little Kiki the monkey loses her way in the jungle. Who will help her find her way home? Hopefully she will find a friend along the way. This fun story teaches the value of friendship and judging people by their actions, not their appearance.

candidate.title: Surprise Island Adventure
candidate.subtitle: Boxcar Children Early Reader (Step into Reading)
candidate.description: The Alden children were searching for a home – and found a life of adventure! Beloved by generations of families, the second book in the Boxcar Children series—adapted into this Step 2 leveled reader—is full of wholesome fun, perfect for children who are learning to read simple sentences. The Aldens spend the summer on Grandfather's island! Joe, the island's friendly handyman, helps them with anything they need, but as the children continue to explore their summer home, they realize there is more to the island―and to their new friend―than meets the eye. Adapted from Gertrude Chandler Warner's Surprise Island chapter book, this early reader allows children to step into reading with a Boxcar Children classic. Step 2 Readers use basic vocabulary and short sentences to tell simple stories. For children who recognize familiar words and can sound out new words with help.

candidate.title: Gabe's Spring Cleaning Adventure - Early Reader - Children's Picture Books
candidate.description: Gabe’s Spring Cleaning Adventure - Early Reader - Children's Picture Books LBugBooks presents our early reader children's picture book series written for preK through 2nd graders. Your beginning reader will enjoy the simple text paired with expressive full-color graphics. All of our stories are sure to delight, inspire, and cause giggling both for you and your child. Follow many of our fun characters as they go through life's adventures from the first day of school to birthday surprises and to places where they have never been. All of our early reader children's picture books teach your child a basic life lesson, such as honesty, friendship, and sharing. Come along with LBugBooks and enjoy a wonderful story your child is sure to read over and over again.

editions.title: The Early Reader in Children’s Literature and Culture

candidate.title: The Early Reader in Children’s Literature and Culture
candidate.subtitle: Theorizing Books for Beginning Readers
candidate.description: This is the first volume to consider the popular literary category of Early Readers – books written and designed for children who are just beginning to read independently. It argues that Early Readers deserve more scholarly attention and careful thought because they are, for many younger readers, their first opportunity to engage with a work of literature on their own, to feel a sense of mastery over a text, and to experience pleasure from the act of reading independently. Using interdisciplinary approaches that draw upon and synthesize research being done in education, child psychology, sociology, cultural studies, and children’s literature, the volume visits Early Readers from a variety of angles: as teaching tools; as cultural artifacts that shape cultural and individual subjectivity; as mass produced products sold to a niche market of parents, educators, and young children; and as aesthetic objects, works of literature and art with specific conventions. Examining the reasons such books are so popular with young readers, as well as the reasons that some adults challenge and censor them, the volume considers the ways Early Readers contribute to the construction of younger children as readers, thinkers, consumers, and as gendered, raced, classed subjects. It also addresses children’s texts that have been translated and sold around the globe, examining them as part of an increasingly transnational children’s media culture that may add to or supplant regional, ethnic, and national children’s literatures and cultures. While this collection focuses mostly on books written in English and often aimed at children living in the US, it is important to acknowledge that these Early Readers are a major US cultural export, influencing the reading habits and development of children across the globe.

candidate.title: Grooty Fledermaus Finds A Friend!
candidate.subtitle: A Read Along Early Reader For Children Ages 4-8
candidate.description: A Read Along Early Reader Book He has no hair! He has no home! Oh my goodness, he's all alone! A wonderful read-along story to share with a young child or grandchild. Born a barn-baby, abandoned and shunned by the other cats for being different, Grooty Fledermaus is a lonely little kitten who has a heart as big as his name but who most certainly won't survive the upcoming winter without help. But when Katie, a young girl who is trying to cope with being ill and who has needs of her own, finds him and rescues him, she has no idea of the astonishing and secret gift that Grooty is about to give her in return! This is 'Book One' in a series of seven books, with each book's plot highlighting an important social lesson. The series uses elements of fantasy for children, as Grooty and Katie experience exciting adventures with trolls, unicorns, dragons, mermaids, reindeer, and best of all, Katie's Grandpa! An adventure book for kids ages 4-8, this picture book is told in easy, pleasing rhyme, and is a story with themes of friendship, being different, and overcoming obstacles. This is a perfect gift if you're looking for stories of imagination and adventure, fun-filled bedtime stories, colourful animal adventure stories, or children's books about illness and overcoming fears. Grooty Fledermaus will be sure to steal the hearts of both young listeners and readers of all ages.

volumeInfo.description: A Read Along Early Reader Book He has no hair! He has no home! Oh my goodness, he's all alone! A wonderful read-along story to share with a young child or grandchild. Born a barn-baby, abandoned and shunned by the other cats for being different, Grooty Fledermaus is a lonely little kitten who has a heart as big as his name but who most certainly won't survive the upcoming winter without help. But when Katie, a young girl who is trying to cope with being ill and who has needs of her own, finds him and rescues him, she has no idea of the astonishing and secret gift that Grooty is about to give her in return! This is 'Book One' in a series of seven books, with each book's plot highlighting an important social lesson. The series uses elements of fantasy for children, as Grooty and Katie experience exciting adventures with trolls, unicorns, dragons, mermaids, reindeer, and best of all, Katie's Grandpa! An adventure book for kids ages 4-8, this picture book is told in easy, pleasing rhyme, and is a story with themes of friendship, being different, and overcoming obstacles. This is a perfect gift if you're looking for stories of imagination and adventure, fun-filled bedtime stories, colourful animal adventure stories, or children's books about illness and overcoming fears. Grooty Fledermaus will be sure to steal the hearts of both young listeners and readers of all ages.

candidate.title: Early Reading and Writing
candidate.subtitle: The Foundations of Literacy
candidate.description: Originally published in 1976. This book is about the teaching of reading and writing in infant schools around Britain for all parents and teachers. It outlines some schemes, approaches, methods and developments but focuses on the pragmatic application. The acquisition and growth of literacy must be seen in the context of the child's total language development, and this book devotes some space to the before-school experience and how parents can help their children.

editions.title: The Adventures of Scooter and Smack Making a Difference

candidate.title: The Adventures of Scooter and Smack Making a Difference
candidate.subtitle: 3 Stories for Early Readers
candidate.description: The Adventures of Scooter and Smack Making a Difference leads children through 3 new stories in this second book in the series, "The Adventures of Scooter and Smack." Simos tells each story using rhyming. Large printed sight words and engaging story lines help children learn to read alone or with an adult. The full-color illustrations show the familiar and lovable characters, Scooter the dog and his best friend Smack, the squirrel, throughout each adventure. Each story brings in new characters to meet, too, including a fox, a skunk, an opossum, and even a grateful spider. The stories, "Scooter and Smack Recycle," "Scooter and Smack Welcome a New Friend," and "Scooter and Smack Return a Lost Treasure" can be enjoyed in any order, so one at a time or all together as a book. Each has a different moral or lesson, but all show the two friends encountering an opportunity to do the right thing as their adventures include "making a difference."

editions.title: Natalie Really Very Much Wants to Be a Star

candidate.title: Natalie Really Very Much Wants to Be a Star
candidate.description: Natalie has big five-year-old dreams for her future. So big, that her heart gets thumpy with excitement. Nat uses her very own words to tell about her hopes, struggles, and adventures. This makes the That's Nat! series perfect for young readers just ready for chapter books. Is There Something Better than Being a Star? Natalie tries out for the lead part in the Sunday school play. But not-so-nice Sasha lands the part instead. Being the star is all Natalie can think about--until something more important catches her heart.

candidate.title: Max E. James
candidate.subtitle: Books 1-3 An Early Reader Chapter Series
candidate.description: This is a collection of the first three books in the Max E. James series printed as one paperback book. Looking for a hilarious, family friendly, early reader chapter series for your independent reader? How about a read aloud bedtime story with short chapters and a few illustrations for your young reader? What if you finally discovered a series that didn't rely on toilet humor or poor language but still entertained? If you're still reading, Max E. James was written for you. Beach Bound: Max can hardly wait for a fun-filled day at the beach with his big brother Cody. But wait-what about that time last summer when Max was bodyboarding, and got totally body-slammed?! Birthday Bash Part I: Max has tossed and turned all night long. He can't wait for his birthday to start. But first, he has to convince his big brother Cody to help him make his birthday wish. Birthday Bash Part II: The birthday festivities have begun and Max E. James can't contain himself! Will his wish finally come true?

candidate.title: Mystery at the Old Ranch
candidate.subtitle: Boxcar Children Early Reader (Step into Reading)
candidate.description: The Alden children were searching for a home–and found a life of adventure! Embark on an exciting journey with the Boxcar Children in this Step 2 leveled reader, as they spend a summer on their aunt's ranch, unraveling mysteries along the way. Perfect for early readers learning to read simple sentences. The Aldens spend the summer on their aunt's ranch! The ranch is a beautiful place, but there are some unusual things happening behind the scenes. As the Boxcar Children explore the land, they make an amazing discovery. Adapted from Gertrude Chandler Warner's Mystery Ranch chapter book, this early reader allows children to start reading with a Boxcar Children classic. Step 2 Readers use basic vocabulary and short sentences to tell simple stories. For children who recognize familiar words and can sound out new words with help.

candidate.title: Meet the Boxcar Children
candidate.subtitle: Boxcar Children Early Reader (Step into Reading)
candidate.description: The Alden children were searching for a home—and found a life of adventure! Beloved by generations of families, the first book in the Boxcar Children series--adapted into this Step 2 leveled reader—is full of wholesome fun, perfect for children who are learning to read simple sentences. Henry, Jessie, Violet, and Benny are brothers and sisters. They're orphans too, and the only way they can stay together is to make it on their own. When the children find an abandoned boxcar in the woods, they decide to call it home—and become the Boxcar Children! Step 2 Readers use basic vocabulary and short sentences to tell simple stories. For children who recognize familiar words and can sound out new words with help.

editions.title: The Early Reader in Children's Literature and Culture

candidate.title: The Early Reader in Children's Literature and Culture
candidate.subtitle: Theorizing Books for Beginning Readers
candidate.description: This is the first volume to consider the popular literary category of Early Readers – books written and designed for children who are just beginning to read independently. It argues that Early Readers deserve more scholarly attention and careful thought because they are, for many younger readers, their first opportunity to engage with a work of literature on their own, to feel a sense of mastery over a text, and to experience pleasure from the act of reading independently. Using interdisciplinary approaches that draw upon and synthesize research being done in education, child psychology, sociology, cultural studies, and children’s literature, the volume visits Early Readers from a variety of angles: as teaching tools; as cultural artifacts that shape cultural and individual subjectivity; as mass produced products sold to a niche market of parents, educators, and young children; and as aesthetic objects, works of literature and art with specific conventions. Examining the reasons such books are so popular with young readers, as well as the reasons that some adults challenge and censor them, the volume considers the ways Early Readers contribute to the construction of younger children as readers, thinkers, consumers, and as gendered, raced, classed subjects. It also addresses children’s texts that have been translated and sold around the globe, examining them as part of an increasingly transnational children’s media culture that may add to or supplant regional, ethnic, and national children’s literatures and cultures. While this collection focuses mostly on books written in English and often aimed at children living in the US, it is important to acknowledge that these Early Readers are a major US cultural export, influencing the reading habits and development of children across the globe.

candidate.title: Joke Book for Kids (Easy Reader)
candidate.subtitle: Illustrated for Ages 5-7: Giggles for Growing Minds
candidate.description: Get ready to laugh out loud with Joke Book for Kids 5-7 (Easy Reader)! This delightful collection of 70 jokes, each paired with its own playful illustration, is perfect for young readers who are just beginning their reading journey. Packed with easy-to-read text and engaging images, this book will have kids giggling in no time. Each joke is carefully crafted to be simple, funny, and entertaining for early readers, making it a great way to encourage a love for reading and spark creativity. From knock knock jokes to animal sounds, this joke book covers all kinds of silly scenarios that kids know and love. What makes this book special? Kid-Friendly Humor: Clean, age-appropriate jokes that are laugh-out-loud funny! Perfect for Early Readers: Simple language that helps kids gain confidence in their reading skills. Playful Illustrations: Fun drawings bring the jokes to life and keep kids engaged. 70 Hilarious Jokes: Each joke is paired with its own delightful image for endless giggles and entertainment. Endless Entertainment: Great for car rides, recess laughs, or family game nights! Whether your child is learning to read or just loves a good laugh, Joke Book for Kids (Easy Reader) is the perfect addition to any young reader's library. Ideal for kids ages 5-7, this book is also a fantastic gift for birthdays, holidays, or just because! Make reading fun-grab your copy today and share the laughs!

raw.title: Joke Book for Kids (Easy Reader)
raw.subtitle: Illustrated for Ages 5-7: Giggles for Growing Minds
raw.description: Get ready to laugh out loud with Joke Book for Kids 5-7 (Easy Reader)! This delightful collection of 70 jokes, each paired with its own playful illustration, is perfect for young readers who are just beginning their reading journey. Packed with easy-to-read text and engaging images, this book will have kids giggling in no time. Each joke is carefully crafted to be simple, funny, and entertaining for early readers, making it a great way to encourage a love for reading and spark creativity. From knock knock jokes to animal sounds, this joke book covers all kinds of silly scenarios that kids know and love. What makes this book special? Kid-Friendly Humor: Clean, age-appropriate jokes that are laugh-out-loud funny! Perfect for Early Readers: Simple language that helps kids gain confidence in their reading skills. Playful Illustrations: Fun drawings bring the jokes to life and keep kids engaged. 70 Hilarious Jokes: Each joke is paired with its own delightful image for endless giggles and entertainment. Endless Entertainment: Great for car rides, recess laughs, or family game nights! Whether your child is learning to read or just loves a good laugh, Joke Book for Kids (Easy Reader) is the perfect addition to any young reader's library. Ideal for kids ages 5-7, this book is also a fantastic gift for birthdays, holidays, or just because! Make reading fun-grab your copy today and share the laughs!

candidate.title: Adventures of a Kid Magician
candidate.subtitle: From the Magical Life of Justin Flom
candidate.description: Adventures of a Kid Magician is Zach's private journal, detailing how magic gets Zach both into and out of trouble as he learns life lessons! Along with Zach's thrilling stories and eye-catching sketches, each adventure comes with a secret magic code that will take you to an exclusive online video of the actual magic tricks used in the adventure! You too will experience the excitement of fooling and impressing your friends with top magician Justin Flom's creative instruction.

editions.title: Magic Book Children's picture book Magic/Fantasy/Adventure

candidate.title: Magic Book Children's picture book Magic/Fantasy/Adventure
candidate.description: "BOOK WITH TEXT IN ENGLISH WRITTEN IN ITALY" "Join Oliver, a resourceful young adventurer, and his friends as they embark on an incredible adventure in the Kingdom of Wonders. Together, they face exciting challenges, meet magical creatures and uncover ancient secrets. Through friendship, courage and Thanks to their ingenuity, Oliver and his friends learn important life lessons and overcome the obstacles that stand in their way. In this extraordinary journey, they will discover the true value of friendship, trust and the importance of following your dreams. gripping and engaging for young readers that will transport them into a world of adventure and magic." WHY MAKE YOUR CHILDREN READ THE BOOK? Your kids will feel drawn into each page, wanting to find out what happens next. The vivid colorful and detailed illustrations will bring the Kingdom of Wonders to life, while the challenging moments and obstacles to overcome will encourage your children to experiment with courage and ingenuity. Together with his trusted friends, Oliver will face mysterious creatures and discover ancient secrets, teaching your children the importance of friendship, trust and perseverance. This book offers a perfect blend of adventure, magic, and moral values, making it an engaging and fun read for your little readers. Whether it's exploring new worlds or tackling personal challenges, your children will be inspired by Oliver's adventures and find important life lessons to accompany them as they grow up. Get ready for an amazing adventure that will spark imaginations and make your kids smile with every page!" BOOK FOR CHILDREN: FROM 6 TO 10 YEARS KINDLE - BOOK NOT RECOMMENDED TO READ ON AN E-READER BOOK - RECOMMENDED TO READ IT ON A TABLET

candidate.title: The Children's Magical Adventures
candidate.description: During the first world war Kenneth Ingle moved his family of mother Gloria, children Asa, Jade and Kaya into the English countryside, but after he becomes a prisoner of the enemy his childrens lives become an adventure after the discovery by them to a world with talking animals. There with the animals help they cross the channel to rescue their father, and in time to help save the animals homes from being buried under concrete by threatened building work.

editions.title: Peter Pan & Other Magical Adventures For Children - 10 Classic Fantasy Books in One Volume (Illustrated Edition)

candidate.title: Peter Pan & Other Magical Adventures For Children - 10 Classic Fantasy Books in One Volume (Illustrated Edition)
candidate.subtitle: A Kiss for Cinderella, Peter Pan in Kensington Gardens, Peter and Wendy, When Wendy Grew Up, The Little White Bird, Sentimental Tommy, Tommy and Grizel, Dear Brutus, Mary Rose…
candidate.description: Musaicum Books presents to you this carefully created volume of "Peter Pan & Other Magical Adventures For Children - 10 Classic Fantasy Books in One Volume (Illustrated Edition)". This ebook has been designed and formatted to the highest digital standards and adjusted for readability on all devices. Contents: Peter Pan Books Peter Pan in Kensington Gardens Peter and Wendy Peter Pan, or The Boy Who Wouldn't Grow Up When Wendy Grew Up Other Books The Little White Bird Sentimental Tommy Tommy and Grizel Dear Brutus A Kiss for Cinderella Mary Rose Sir James Matthew Barrie (1860-1937) was a Scottish novelist and playwright, best remembered today as the creator of Peter Pan. He was born and educated in Scotland but moved to London, where he met the Llewelyn Davies boys, who inspired him to write about a baby boy who has magical adventures in Kensington Gardens, then to write Peter Pan, or The Boy Who Wouldn't Grow Up, a "fairy play" about an ageless boy and an ordinary girl named Wendy who have adventures in the fantasy setting of Neverland.

candidate.title: Barbie's Magical Adventure in Dreamland
candidate.description: Reading "Barbie's Magical Adventure in Dreamland" takes people to magical worlds of thought and wonder. The story is about Barbie, a young girl with beautiful golden hair and bright blue eyes who stands out because she can go to Dreamland, a magical place that only lives in her dreams. Barbie goes on exciting adventures, meets strange creatures, and discovers beautiful landscapes in Dreamland every night under the stars. These dreams turn into her favorite secret, a beautiful world she loves. Dreamland's magic starts to fade away one day, leaving the once-bright colors dull and the magical creatures dull. Dreamland's guardian, Cygnus, calls on Barbie to protect this magical paradise. The magic of Dreamland comes from the three Dream Crystals that are lost. It is her job to find them. Barbie starts her trip with a purse full of stardust and an unknown map. She goes on a journey through the Whispering Woods, where the trees tell her secrets. To find the Dream Crystals that are hidden there, she has to answer puzzles. Barbie will have to show how brave and dedicated she is when she jumps into the Enchanted Lake to find the last crystal. She faces her biggest fears and gives the stones back to Cygnus, protecting Dreamland's magic and making sure it will live on. When Barbie goes back to her quiet small town, she grows into a kind and inspiring person. Kids and adults alike are inspired by her adventures in Dreamland and how she lives out these ideals to enjoy the beauty of dreams and grow the traits of bravery, kindness, and curiosity. At the end of the story, Barbie is still Dreamland's keeper. She watches over its wonders and makes sure that people will love them for many more years. She closes her eyes and dreams of Dreamland all the time because she knows that the fun things, she did there will always be a part of her. "Barbie's Magical Adventure in Dreamland" shows how powerful dreams, kindness, and bravery can be. It reminds us that the world is full of amazing things just waiting to be found by people who believe, and that the keys to amazing experiences and lasting memories are inside our hearts. Story Begins: Barbie was a little girl who lived in a cute little town. People knew her for having beautiful golden hair, bright blue eyes, and a kind heart. Barbie stood out among the simple homes and friendly neighbors in the town as a symbol of modesty and purity. Barbie had golden hair that fell down her back in waves that sparkled like a river of light. As the light caught on her and turned into a beautiful halo, she looked like a living, breathing sign of hope. Her bright blue eyes sparkled with interest, like two sapphires watching over the secrets of the world. But what made Barbie different from the other kids in town was her heart. She had a giving heart that touched everyone she met. She could make anyone feel special and better, even on the worst days, with her kind words and warm smile. It was Barbie's job to show that even in a world that seemed rude and rushed, good manners could still win. The one thing that made Barbie different, though, was something that not many people knew about her: she could go to Dreamland, a magical world that appeared only in her dreams. The hamlet would go to sleep every night, and Barbie's travels would begin. Dreamland was a place where dream rules did not apply. It stretched out in front of her like a beautiful tapestry in her thoughts. It was a world where the trees whispered secrets and the animals spoke in riddles and wisecracks. The sky was purple and turquoise. In Dreamland, Barbie can be anyone and go anywhere. It's a place where anything is possible and where the soul can find comfort. Her adventures at night were amazing. She would soar through the sky on a shiny dragon, feeling the rush of freedom in her blood as the wind pulled her hair back. She would have tea parties with rabbits that could talk. The rabbits' laughter would sound like a happy symphony in a field of brightly colored flowers. She liked to wander around in beautiful forests where streams sang lullabies to long-lost hopes and trees whispered stories of past adventures. Barbie, on the other hand, found the animals in Dreamland to be very interesting. There was Lumina, a pretty fairy whose wings sparkled like stars at dusk. She would teach Barbie about the night sky by telling her stories about constellations and the music that the stars played. Nimbus, a sneaky cloud fairy who can change shape at will, came next. Nimbus could change into a dolphin to explore the depths of the dream seas or a kite to take Barbie high above the clouds. Barbie and Nimbus would go on exciting adventures together. She made new friends every night, and each one was more creative than the last. She laughed with kind giants whose hearts were as big as the sky, danced with fire sprites whose bright flames lit up the night sky, and talked with wise old owls who taught her ancient secrets.

Her adventures at night were amazing. She would soar through the sky on a shiny dragon, feeling the rush of freedom in her blood as the wind pulled her hair back. She would have tea parties with rabbits that could talk. The rabbits' laughter would sound like a happy symphony in a field of brightly colored flowers. She liked to wander around in beautiful forests where streams sang lullabies to long-lost hopes and trees whispered stories of past adventures. Barbie, on the other hand, found the animals in Dreamland to be very interesting. There was Lumina, a pretty fairy whose wings sparkled like stars at dusk. She would teach Barbie about the night sky by telling her stories about constellations and the music that the stars played. Nimbus, a sneaky cloud fairy who can change shape at will, came next. Nimbus could change into a dolphin to explore the depths of the dream seas or a kite to take Barbie high above the clouds. Barbie and Nimbus would go on exciting adventures together. She made new friends every night, and each one was more creative than the last. She laughed with kind giants whose hearts were as big as the sky, danced with fire sprites whose bright flames lit up the night sky, and talked with wise old owls who taught her ancient secrets.

editions.title: MERRIMEG - Seven Magical Adventures for Children

candidate.title: MERRIMEG - Seven Magical Adventures for Children
candidate.description: Once upon a time there was a little girl whose name was Merrimeg. Sometimes she was good, and sometimes she was, well, not so good. Whatever the situation she found herself in, or managed to get herself into, she was always merry and happy - if not always obedient. In this illustrated book are 7 magical adventures on which Merrimeg goes. They are: Merrimeg and The Chimney Imps Merrimeg and The Clop-Clop Shoes Merrimeg and The Starlight Fairies Merrimeg and The Echo Dwarfs Merrimeg and The Rag-Bone Man Merrimeg and The Apple-Seed Elf Merrimeg and The May-Dew 10% of the profit from the sale of this book will be donated to charities. =========== KEYWORDS/TAGS: Folklore, fairytales, myths, legends, fairy tales, fables, childrens stories, bedtime stories, air, angry, Apple-Seed, arms, asleep, beautiful, blackbird, bottom, butterfly, caterpillar, cave, children, chimney imps, crying, Elf, echo, fairies, Florrie, forest, garden, gnomes, handkerchief, Hark, ladder, laughter, Malkin, Merrimeg, Myrma, Nibby, Painter, Pennie, Peter, ponies, Rag-and-Bone, Sappy, trees, village, walked, waterfall, white, wings, Winnie, starlight, clop-clop, dwarfs, may-dew

editions.title: Magical Adventures. A Collection of Enchanting Stories for Children.

candidate.title: Magical Adventures. A Collection of Enchanting Stories for Children.
candidate.description: Magical Adventure is a collection of enchanting stories for children.Sonia Jones, Magical Adventures for Children is a beautifully crafted fantasy storybook that will take the year readers on an enchanting journey they will never forget. The story follows several characters and wondrous worlds filled with fantastical creatures and an exciting adventure. In this world you will meet a talking Unicorn, Fairies, Dragons, mermaids, and a giant to name but a few. The story is filled with magic, danger, and thrilling challenges that will keep the young reader engaged from start to finish. Sonia Jones's writing is creative and evocative, and the book is beautifully illustrated with stunning visuals that bring the stories to life. Along with many characters, readers will learn important lessons about bravery, perseverance, and the power of imagination. Magical Adventures of Children is an excellent choice for parents and educators who want to encourage creativity and a love of reading in children. It is a must-read for any young reader who loves fantasy and adventure stories.

candidate.title: Magical Madness
candidate.subtitle: A Mystery Adventure Book for Kids Aged 9-15
candidate.description: Magic. Mayhem. And Nerf crossbows. Welcome to Magical Madness. If you like wizards, dragons, knights and magical adventures, you'll love this ...Thomas Tinkler is ten years old. He's too mature for a trip to the soft play centre, in his opinion. He's adamant that he won't go. But his parents insist he goes anyway, because his younger brother, Finlay, needs to burn off some energy. When they arrive, they see that the play centre is no longer called Tumble Kids. It's been taken over by a mysterious man, who's rebranded the place as MAGICAL MADNESS.Once inside, the boys meet some new friends, Brady and Ella, who help them explore. They enjoy playing together. They chase each other down padded aisles. They race down slides and muck about in the ball pool. They even have a friendly contest, trying to knock each other off a balance beam with foam axes. All is going well - until Thomas discovers a door that leads to another part of the centre. And that's when the real adventure begins ...

candidate.title: The Magical Adventures
candidate.subtitle: A Imaginative Short Story Book For Kids Ages 5-10
candidate.description: The Magical Adventures is a whimsical storybook that takes young readers on a thrilling journey to a world of wonder and magic. Siblings Luna and Leo discover a mysterious book that transports them to enchanted forests, hidden caves, and even a sea of stars. Along the way, they meet fascinating creatures like fairies, giants, dragons, and mermaids. Through rhythmic storytelling and enchanting illustrations, The Magical Adventures of Luna and Leo captures the imagination of young readers and encourages them to explore the magic that lies within themselves. Luna and Leo learn important lessons about courage, kindness, and friendship, and inspire readers to embrace the wonder of the world around them. This captivating tale is perfect for children aged 5-10 who love to use their imaginations and go on exciting adventures. "The Magical Adventures of Luna and Leo" is a timeless story that will inspire and delight young readers for generations to come.

candidate.title: Adventure in Magical Forest
candidate.subtitle: A Adventure Story Magical for Kids Ages 6-10
candidate.description: Welcome to an adventure in a magical forest. Join Lila, Max and Jack on an exciting adventure through a magical forest, where they will discover magical objects and face an evil goblin. Do you think they manage to pass the tests and defeat the bad goblin? This story is great for boys and girls ages 6 to 10 who love exciting, magical adventures. It has a powerful message about the value of friendship and perseverance. This book will become a family favorite. You can not lose this! Immerse yourself in this fascinating story full of action, emotion and magic.

candidate.title: Kids Magical Stories
candidate.subtitle: Amazing Adventure For Children
candidate.description: "Kids Magical Stories" is a wonderful book filled with amazing adventures for children to enjoy. Inside this book, you will find magical tales where extraordinary things happen, and the characters go on exciting journeys. Join brave knights and curious princesses as they explore mysterious forests and ancient castles. Discover talking animals, fairies, and mythical creatures in magical lands. Meet friendly ghosts, superhero pets, and mischievous genies with special powers. In these stories, kids will learn important lessons while having fun. They will visit magical schools and secret gardens, and travel through time with inventive characters. Each story is crafted with imagination and love, taking readers on thrilling and heartwarming adventures. "Kids Magical Stories" is all about using your imagination, making friends, and believing in the magic of the world. Each story will inspire young minds to dream big and embrace their uniqueness. It's a perfect book to read before bedtime or during a cozy afternoon. Let these enchanting tales spark your imagination and take you on a journey full of laughter, wonder, and joy. Dive into the world of magic, where anything is possible, and dreams can come true. Enjoy the adventures and the magic of storytelling in "Kids Magical Stories."

candidate.title: Children's Books and Their Creators
candidate.description: Unique in its coverage of contemporary American children's literature, this timely, single-volume reference covers the books our children are--or should be--reading now, from board books to young adult novels. Enriched with dozens of color illustrations and the voices of authors and illustrators themselves, it is a cornucopia of delight. 23 color, 153 b&w illustrations.

candidate.title: The Enchanted Pages
candidate.subtitle: A Magical Adventure Story for Kids About Friendship, Imagination, and the Power of Reading
candidate.description: What if opening a book could pull you inside its story? On a rainy afternoon, four curious friends-Lena, Vihaan, Oliver, and Sophie-discover a mysterious golden book hidden deep inside their old library. The moment they open it, they are transported into a breathtaking magical world where stories come alive. In the Land of Stories, talking trees whisper secrets, riddles guard ancient bridges, giant fish test clever minds, and a dark ruler known as the Shadow King controls the fate of imagination itself. Guided by a living book, the children must journey together to retrieve the lost Quill of Imagination-before the final page turns forever. The Enchanted Pages is a heartwarming fantasy adventure that celebrates: The joy of reading and storytelling Friendship, teamwork, and courage Creativity and imagination Problem-solving through wisdom, not force Perfect for bedtime reading, classroom storytelling, and independent young readers, this book inspires children to believe in the magic of books-and themselves. ✨ A must-have addition to every child's bookshelf.

editions.title: The Mystery of the Whispering Woods

candidate.title: The Mystery of the Whispering Woods
candidate.subtitle: A Magical Adventure Book for Kids
candidate.description: Dive into Adventure with "The Mystery of the Whispering Woods" - Book 1 in The Legends of Lunaria Series Unlock the secrets of the Whispering Woods in this thrilling first installment of the "Legends of Lunaria" series! Ideal for young readers aged 6-10, this magical adventure book blends mystery, friendship, and a dash of magic, captivating both children and parents. Why You'll Love "The Legends of Lunaria" Engaging Adventure: Follow four young heroes as they explore the mystical Lunaria, a land filled with magical creatures and ancient secrets. Inspiring Characters: Meet Mason, Mia, Anika, and Ethan - children just like yours who discover bravery and friendship on their quest to save their world. Vivid Imagery: Delight in the enchanted forests with glowing leaves and mystical creatures, brought to life through vibrant illustrations. Life Lessons: Learn about courage, loyalty, and the power of unity to overcome challenges. Perfect for Young Readers: Embark on a journey where each page holds a new thrill! Ideal for children who love stories filled with fantasy, adventure, and mystery. "The Legends of Lunaria" isn't just a book series -it's a gateway to a world of imagination and discovery.

editions.title: Zane Andree and the Dragon Machine

candidate.title: Zane Andree and the Dragon Machine
candidate.subtitle: Storybook
candidate.description: Zane Andree and the Dragon Machine A Magical Dragon Adventure for Kids What happens when a tiny baby dragon meets a mischievous villain and a mysterious machine? Join Zane Andree, a brave young hero, and his clever monkey Hogatika on an exciting adventure in Dragon Valley! When the sneaky villain Zaza steals a sweet baby dragon and places it inside his strange Dragon Machine, something incredible happens - the tiny dragon suddenly grows into a huge, powerful dragon! Zaza thinks he has created the most powerful dragon in the world. But Zane and Hogatika discover a surprising secret that could save the dragon and stop Zaza's plan. With courage, teamwork, and a little kindness, they must help the dragon remember who it really is. Will they stop Zaza before it's too late? Kids Will Love This Story Because It Has: Dragons and magical creatures A funny and brave monkey sidekick A young hero kids can cheer for Bright, colorful illustrations A heartwarming lesson about kindness Perfect For - Kids ages 3-8 - Bedtime stories - Early readers - Families who love dragon adventures Follow Zane Andree, Hogatika, and their dragon friend in a magical journey where bravery and kindness save the day!

editions.title: The Early Reader in Children’s Literature and Culture

candidate.title: The Early Reader in Children’s Literature and Culture
candidate.subtitle: Theorizing Books for Beginning Readers
candidate.description: This is the first volume to consider the popular literary category of Early Readers – books written and designed for children who are just beginning to read independently. It argues that Early Readers deserve more scholarly attention and careful thought because they are, for many younger readers, their first opportunity to engage with a work of literature on their own, to feel a sense of mastery over a text, and to experience pleasure from the act of reading independently. Using interdisciplinary approaches that draw upon and synthesize research being done in education, child psychology, sociology, cultural studies, and children’s literature, the volume visits Early Readers from a variety of angles: as teaching tools; as cultural artifacts that shape cultural and individual subjectivity; as mass produced products sold to a niche market of parents, educators, and young children; and as aesthetic objects, works of literature and art with specific conventions. Examining the reasons such books are so popular with young readers, as well as the reasons that some adults challenge and censor them, the volume considers the ways Early Readers contribute to the construction of younger children as readers, thinkers, consumers, and as gendered, raced, classed subjects. It also addresses children’s texts that have been translated and sold around the globe, examining them as part of an increasingly transnational children’s media culture that may add to or supplant regional, ethnic, and national children’s literatures and cultures. While this collection focuses mostly on books written in English and often aimed at children living in the US, it is important to acknowledge that these Early Readers are a major US cultural export, influencing the reading habits and development of children across the globe.

editions.title: The Great Magic LEM Three Friends and Their Journey

candidate.title: The Great Magic LEM Three Friends and Their Journey
candidate.subtitle: A Tale about how Life's Magic Can Make Dreams Come True.
candidate.description: An unforgettable dragon adventure filled with courage, friendship, and magic, perfect for kids ages 6-12. Step into a magical world where a brave boy, a clever girl, and a goodhearted dragon set out on a journey that will test their hearts, their choices, and their courage. "The Great Magic LEM: Three Friends and Their Journey" is a beautifully illustrated fantasy book for kids, designed especially for young readers who love adventure but also need a story with meaning. Fully illustrated on every page Brings the story to life and keeps children engaged from beginning to end. Large print & easy to read Perfect for early readers, reluctant readers, and comfortable read-aloud sessions. A powerful story about courage and friendship Teaches important life lessons in a natural, engaging way. Light humor and fun moments Just enough laughter to keep the adventure exciting. Perfect for ages 6-12 Great for independent reading or sharing together. A story with heart This is more than just a dragon book for kids; it's a journey about: making the right choices believing in yourself helping others discovering what true courage means Perfect for young readers who enjoy illustrated chapter books fantasy adventure stories books about friendship and kindness easy-to-read large-print books magical stories with meaningful lessons Whether your child is just starting their reading journey or already loves fantasy adventures, this heartwarming dragon story will spark imagination and leave a lasting impression.

candidate.title: The Sleepy Little Dragon
candidate.subtitle: A Bedtime Adventure for Little Dragons
candidate.description: ﻿Join Cedric, the little dragon who just can’t seem to stay awake! From morning playtime to bedtime stories, Cedric discovers that even the tiniest dragons need rest, friendship, and a little bit of adventure. Packed with bright, colorful illustrations, this bedtime story is perfect for children ages 2–7 who love dragons, fun adventures, and magical tales. Parents and young readers will enjoy: Simple, easy-to-read text for early readers Fun illustrations that bring each scene to life A heartwarming story about rest, courage, and friendship Perfect for bedtime reading or quiet storytime, The Sleepy Little Dragon is a magical addition to any child’s bookshelf!

candidate.title: Sparky Dragon Tales
candidate.subtitle: First Flight: A Tale of Courage and Adventure
candidate.description: Discover the Magic of Perseverance with Sparky! Join Sparky, the little dragon with big dreams, in his first exhilarating adventure from "Sparky's Dragon Tales" series. In "First Flight" young readers will soar into a world where dragons fill the sky and dreams are as high as the clouds. About the Book: Sparky is not your average dragon. Born with wings too small to fly, he's determined to prove that anyone can overcome their limitations with a little hard work and a lot of heart. With his vibrant green scales and a spirit that can't be grounded, Sparky embarks on a quest to take to the skies. Alongside his loving and wise mother, and a cast of colorful dragon friends, Sparky learns the true meaning of perseverance and teamwork. For Parents and Educators: "First Flight" is crafted to teach children about determination, resilience, and the importance of following one's dreams, making it a perfect addition to any young reader's library. This book is an excellent resource for teaching early learners about overcoming obstacles and the value of hard work. Why You'll Love This Book: Engaging Storyline: Captivates kids with fun, adventure, and memorable characters. Beautiful Illustrations: Brings the story to life with vibrant art that will keep children coming back for more. Valuable Lessons: Teaches important life skills through a narrative that children can relate to. Perfect for All Ages: Ideal for reading aloud or for young readers to explore on their own. Grab your copy today and join Sparky as he discovers that with courage and persistence, the sky's the limit!

candidate.title: Dragon Book for Kids
candidate.subtitle: Diary of a Friendly Dragon (Adventure Stories for Kids)
candidate.description: Dragons and humans... can they live together? An epic tale/tail of dragons and humans that can't seem to get along, but this time, it's all from the dragon's perspective. It's a reversed world with ethics being discussed. Conversations about peace or war rage in the hearts of the giant beasts. Will the main character find the mercy and courage he is looking for to connect with the humans and team up to defeat the real enemy?

candidate.title: The Great Magic Ena
candidate.subtitle: A Helping Hand and a Dragon Story: A Tale of Courage, Curiosity, and the Magic of Learning Together.
candidate.description: Two unusual friends, a dragon named Solomat and a boy named Gruia, were exploring a beautiful valley in Ler when they rescued Ena from a trap. Like Gruia, Ena loved nature and dreamed of learning more about it through alchemy. She shared an intriguing story passed down from her grandfather, the tale of an ancient, wise dragon who could teach her the secrets of the craft. Fascinated, Solomat and Gruia chose to accompany her to the legendary dragon master's realm, embarking on a journey filled with friendship, trust, gratitude, and knowledge. The story shows that friendship and working together can make a difference and that being kind and helpful to others can help you grow and see things in a new way.

raw.title: The Great Magic Ena
raw.subtitle: A Helping Hand and a Dragon Story: A Tale of Courage, Curiosity, and the Magic of Learning Together.
raw.description: Two unusual friends, a dragon named Solomat and a boy named Gruia, were exploring a beautiful valley in Ler when they rescued Ena from a trap. Like Gruia, Ena loved nature and dreamed of learning more about it through alchemy. She shared an intriguing story passed down from her grandfather, the tale of an ancient, wise dragon who could teach her the secrets of the craft. Fascinated, Solomat and Gruia chose to accompany her to the legendary dragon master's realm, embarking on a journey filled with friendship, trust, gratitude, and knowledge. The story shows that friendship and working together can make a difference and that being kind and helpful to others can help you grow and see things in a new way.

candidate.title: Fuzzwippers Make Great Travelers
candidate.description: Winner of the Mom's Choice Award for excellence in family friendly media, Fuzzwippers Make Great Travelers empowers children to explore our world as they open their minds and hearts to embrace diversity and other cultures. The Fuzzwipper series includes tips for children and a parent's pointer too. The Fuzzwipper series promotes self-esteem, socio-emotional health, and life skills for children through the power of story. Fuzzany and Alexandra travel to Hawaii and learn that trying new things means making mistakes but that if you never give up, you will learn a little about a new skill, and a lot about yourself.

He discovers that the things he enjoys doing most are gifts from God -- gifts with a purpose and a perfect plan ... straight from heaven.

candidate.title: Dodo la planète do, 2
candidate.description: Beautifully recorded lullabies from around the world are sung in the language of origin along with an illustrated bedtime story--in English, Spanish, and French--about an imaginary journey to unknown lands. Performers Sylva Balassanian, Mary Fujiwara Burke, Juan Jose Carranza, Hart-Rouge, H'sao, Paul Kunigis, and Nathalie Picard share their culture in offering lovely, warm lead vocals and harmonies, supported by a highly skilled group of musicians playing more than 30 different instruments. The earth-toned, dreamlike illustrations reflect both the subject and country of origin of each song.

raw.description: Beautifully recorded lullabies from around the world are sung in the language of origin along with an illustrated bedtime story--in English, Spanish, and French--about an imaginary journey to unknown lands. Performers Sylva Balassanian, Mary Fujiwara Burke, Juan Jose Carranza, Hart-Rouge, H'sao, Paul Kunigis, and Nathalie Picard share their culture in offering lovely, warm lead vocals and harmonies, supported by a highly skilled group of musicians playing more than 30 different instruments. The earth-toned, dreamlike illustrations reflect both the subject and country of origin of each song.

editions.title: Ghost Cat Who Saved My Life

candidate.title: Ghost Cat Who Saved My Life
candidate.description: Meow! Meow! Meow! The flat upstairs from Sav is empty, so who or what is making the strange yowling noises she can hear at night? Could it be a ghost cat? Sav and her best friend Liam decide to investigate, but nothing is quite what it seems on this very surprising ghost hunt!

candidate.title: Halfway to Happily Ever After
candidate.description: When fairy godmother training is complicated by half the trainees going on strike, Isabelle, who doesn't want to let her skills get rusty, decides to help Nora after learning she's unhappy at summer camp.

candidate.title: Dixie's Chicken Sisters
candidate.subtitle: How a Curious Young Girl Learned to Care for a Free-Range Flock
candidate.description: "Have you ever been to a farm supply store during Chick Days? That's where my life began! It was crowded and noisy there for those few weeks. Luckily, after many adventures in my first year, I found my permanent home just north of Canton, Missouri. That's where I met Zita Robertson, and she helped me finally get my wings on a typewriter. I have filled this entire book with my stories and I have more to say! I hope you'll enjoy learning about me and my sisters."

candidate.title: Oceans and Seas
candidate.description: The biggest, fastest, scariest, weirdest . . . most AMAZING sea creatures! Learn all about the most incredible animals to ever swim in the sea. Fascinating facts, ingenious folding gatefolds and super-sized action flaps bring these weird and wonderful, dazzling and deadly creatures to life.
